Title: Beijing says US Tiananmen comments 'smear' China
Article:
BEIJING: China accused the United States on Thursday of distorting facts and smearing its political system, after Secretary of State Marco Rubio said censorship could not "erase" the memory of Beijing's 1989 Tiananmen crackdown.
On June 4 that year, the Chinese government sent troops and tanks to crush protests calling for political reform in and around Beijing's Tiananmen Square.
The death toll remains unknown, and discussion of what happened is censored in mainland China.
Rubio told a news conference on Wednesday that "no amount of censorship can erase the past."
"Those who sacrificed to uphold their unalienable rights of free expression and peaceful assembly will be vindicated someday," he said.
China's foreign ministry said Thursday it firmly opposed Rubio's comments.
"The Chinese government has long since reached a clear conclusion regarding that political turmoil that occurred in the late 1980s," ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning told a regular news briefing.
"The relevant erroneous remarks by the US side distort historical facts, smear China's political system and development path, and interfere in China's internal affairs," she said.
This year, authorities reportedly prevented the families of those who died in 1989 from visiting their graves at Beijing's Wan'an Cemetery, with Amnesty International calling the move "a heartless act."
Beijing has also moved in recent years to snuff out all public commemorations in Hong Kong, where an annual candlelight vigil had been held for decades before the imposition of a national security law in 2020.
AFP reporters saw a heavy police presence on Wednesday near Hong Kong's Victoria Park, the former site of the event.
Late that night, activist Tang Ngok-kwan stood alone in the park, reading the names of hundreds of victims in a low voice under the watchful eyes of several plainclothes police officers.
Derek Chu, a former district councillor who has been giving out free candles in his shop every anniversary since 2022, told AFP that "the space for (free) speech is more and more narrow."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dancer charged with sexually abusing Malaysian teen in Beijing
Article:
SEREMBAN: A freelance dancer claimed trial at the Sessions Court here today to charges of physical sexual abuse and committing carnal intercourse against the order of nature on a 16-year-old Malaysian boy in Beijing, China, last year.
Muhammad Nazri Lanibi, 32, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read out to him before Sessions Court judge Shahrul Rizal Majid.
For the first charge, the accused is alleged to have intentionally committed carnal intercourse against the order of nature without consent by performing oral sex on the 16-year-old boy.
The offence was allegedly committed about 12.30am at the China National Convention Centre Grand Hotel in Beijing, China, in June last year.
If convicted, he faces up to 20 years in jail and whipping.
On the second charge, Muhammad Nazri is accused of committing physical sexual abuse against the same teenager at the same location about 12.30am on June 20 and 21 last year.
The charge carries a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Zul Azrin Zainuddin proposed bail at RM20,000 for each charge.
However, defence lawyer Mohd Hanif Hassan pleaded for a minimum bail amount, saying that his client is supporting his sister, who suffers from cancer, and a mentally disabled nephew.
The court subsequently set bail at RM14,000 for both charges and fixed July 6 for case mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israel: Any Hizbollah attack could bring strikes on Beirut
Article:
JERUSALEM: Israel's defence minister said Thursday that a ceasefire agreement with Lebanon grants the military the "freedom" to strike Beirut if Hizbollah attacks Israeli communities, adding that operations in southern Lebanon would continue.
"The IDF will, at this stage, continue its fire and ground operations, remain in the security zone in Lebanon up to the Yellow Line – including in the Beaufort area – and without the return of the population, while continuing to dismantle infrastructure on the ground," Israel Katz said in a statement, as he hailed the ceasefire deal reached on Wednesday.
Katz said Israeli forces retained the "freedom of action, with American backing, to strike in Beirut in response to fire on Israeli communities and territory."
Israel has established a so-called Yellow Line inside Lebanese territory about a dozen kilometres from its northern border since an initial ceasefire came into effect on April 17.
Katz said the latest ceasefire agreement, brokered by Washington, "unequivocally" calls for disarming Hizbollah across Lebanon.
The ceasefire stipulates the "removal of Hizbollah from the entire area south of the Litani River and the creation of a demilitarised zone" there, he added.
In a separate statement, the Israeli military's Arabic-language spokesman, Colonel Avichay Adraee, warned residents in southern Lebanon to "refrain from heading south of the Zahrani River until further notice."
"The fighting in southern Lebanon continues as the Israel Defence Forces continue to target Hizbollah facilities and infrastructure located in your villages and nearby," he said on X.
Last week, Israel's military declared all areas south of Lebanon's Zahrani River – around 40km from the border and including the cities of Tyre and Nabatieh – to be "combat zones" and told residents to evacuate.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dalai Lama, 90, to seek knee treatment in New Delhi
Article:
NEW DELHI: The Dalai Lama will receive medical treatment on his left knee in New Delhi this month, his office said on Thursday, as the 90-year-old deals with a series of health issues.
The spiritual head of Tibetan Buddhists is due to travel to the Indian capital on Friday from his home-in-exile in the Himalayan town of Dharamshala.
His followers, concerned about his health in recent years, typically hold elaborate prayers ahead of any planned medical procedures.
"He will undergo medical treatment on his left knee," the Dalai Lama's office said on X, adding that he was expected to travel to India's Ladakh region by the end of June for an extended stay.
The Nobel Peace Prize winner had surgery on the same knee in New York in 2024, though he told Reuters months later it was "nothing serious", despite walking gingerly with the help of aides.
He now uses a golf cart for longer distances within his compound.
The 14th Dalai Lama said last year he hoped to live beyond 130 years, extending his earlier prediction, and has reassured followers that he will be reincarnated after his death.
The longest-serving head of Tibetan Buddhism has lived in Dharamshala since fleeing Tibet in 1959 following a failed uprising against Chinese rule.
China, which seeks to consolidate its control over Tibet, regards the Dalai Lama as a separatist and says it must approve his successor, citing a centuries-old ritual.
The Dalai Lama has urged his followers to reject anyone chosen by Beijing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Group wants MACC to probe transfer of elephants to Japan zoo
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A wildlife rights group has urged the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission to probe the transfer of three elephants from Malaysia to a zoo in Japan, citing unanswered questions over the matter.
Hak Asasi Hidupan Liar Malaysia (Hidup) lawyer Rajesh Nagarajan said the public deserved to know if payments were involved in the relocation of the elephants - Dara, Amoi and Kelat - to Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, and if public funds were used.
At a press conference, Rajesh claimed a document issued by a company showed the cost of transporting the elephants was RM368,250.
"We want to know who paid. Did Malaysia pay for the animals to go there? Or did Japan pay? Is this our taxpayers' money? We have a right to know. If it is our taxpayers' money, then why are we paying for our elephants to go overseas?" he said.
Rajesh said documents obtained from a whistleblower showed that the elephants were classified under Appendix I of the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora. He claimed this meant they could be taken out of the country.
He also alleged that veterinary certification documents for the elephants were signed by authorities in Pahang, despite public information indicating that the elephants came from Perak's Taiping Zoo.
"If the elephants were in Taiping, why was the certification issued by a Pahang veterinary officer? It does not make sense and the authorities must explain this discrepancy," he said.
Activist Shereen Yunos, meanwhile, said they received a report on the status of the animals from elephant conservation specialist Dr William Keith Lindsay on May 21.
She said Lindsay's report indicated that the elephants were showing signs of physical and psychological stress, including exhibiting repetitive behaviour.
Shereen also claimed the elephant enclosure at Tennoji Zoo was only 1,612 square metres, far below standards in other countries, like the minimum 20,000 square metres set for the same number of elephants in Great Britain.
"We want the three elephants returned to Malaysia. They are part of our national heritage and deserve proper care in an environment suited to their welfare," she said on the elephants that were relocated on March 11.
The issue has drawn increasing scrutiny in recent months.
Previously, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the return of the
elephants should be considered
if the arrangement was found to be inconsistent with conservation principles, animal welfare standards and current sensitivities.
Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail has also called for the
elephants to be returned
and placed at the National Elephant Conservation Centre in Kuala Gandah.
The New Straits Times is reaching out to the Taiping Zoo and the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Jobs for veterans by Transport, Defence ministries not tied to polls
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A joint initiative by the Transport and Defence ministries to provide jobs for armed forces veterans is unrelated to upcoming elections, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
He said the initiative, which offers 3,208 job opportunities in the transport and logistics sectors, aims to safeguard the welfare of military retirees.
"This is not related to the Johor election, and it is also not about UMDAP. It is a commitment to deliver the national agenda," he said on the sidelines of the launch ceremony today, alluding to a term used by critics regarding Umno's ties with DAP in the unity government.
"The initiative reflects the government's concern for all segments of society, particularly those in the military who are approaching retirement," he added.
Present were Transport Minister Anthony Loke and Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin.
The collaboration will also streamline the conversion of military driving licences to Class E licences for heavy vehicles.
Zahid said the high level of discipline among military personnel would benefit industries through technical skills, dedication, and focus.
He said the inter-ministerial collaboration would produce faster results by avoiding silo-based approaches.
"We are looking at a short-service retirement age for officers and personnel, who are important human capital and workforce."
"This is because TVET is now a preferred pathway within the education ecosystem, and we must not place this responsibility on those who have not gone through the traditional education system," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt expands Rahmah sales to buffer food price hikes
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The government is expanding the Jualan Rahmah Madani initiatives, as it moves to cushion the expected rise in food prices from June onwards.
This allows Malaysians to continue to buy essential goods at prices 10 to 30 per cent below market rates.
Federal Agricultural Marketing Authority director-general Abdul Rashid Bahri said the programme, run jointly with the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, remains a key intervention to keep household expenses manageable while allowing market prices to adjust.
"Together with the Domestic Trade Ministry, we have the Ramah sales. Through this cooperation, we ensure the prices offered are 10 to 30 per cent lower than market prices," he told newsmen here today.
He was met after the launch of the new Kopiesatu outlet at Trend Mart and Cafe in Komtar here by Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow.
Abdul Rashid said the initiative was part of a broader rollout of direct-to-consumer food programmes, including Agro Madani sales, aimed at narrowing the gap between farm gate prices and retail costs amid global and seasonal price pressures.
He said that a moderate increase in food prices is expected from June, but added that government mechanisms are in place to prevent sharp spikes that could strain consumers.
"We cannot say prices will not go up. What we ensure is that the increase remains within affordability," he said.
Around 5,000 Agro Madani sales events are conducted annually nationwide, involving Fama, as well as its partners.
Nearly 1,000 events have already been carried out in the first two months of the year, reflecting strong demand for lower-priced essentials.
Abdul Rashid said the sustained turnout showed that direct sales remain an effective tool in easing cost-of-living pressures, particularly for fresh produce and daily necessities.
On food security, Abdul Rashid said there is currently no requirement for a formal stockpiling mechanism under Fama, as overall supply conditions remain stable across the country.
"At the moment, we are not hearing much about shortages. Eggs, chicken — all are sufficient," he said, adding that Fama would step in at specific localities if any supply disruptions occur.
Beyond price stabilisation efforts, Fama is also expanding its entrepreneur development push through the Trend Mart and Cafe concept, which operates under a licensing model rather than traditional franchising.
It was reported that the government expected food prices to continue rising in the second half of this year as the ongoing global energy crisis impacted shipping costs, including for fertilisers and animal feed.
Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said the government had already begun
implementing preliminary measures
and initiatives to mitigate the anticipated price hikes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Facing plunging support, will Netanyahu agree to a tougher Lebanon stance?
Article:
ISRAELI Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu faces plunging support in the electorally vital north where Hizbollah rocket fire has been heaviest, a new poll has shown, putting pressure on him to take a more hawkish stance as elections loom.
Wednesday night's ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon - whether it holds or not - may not be what northern voters have in mind.
The May poll by Agam Labs at Israel's Hebrew University, shared exclusively with Reuters, showed residents in the north abandoning Netanyahu's Likud more quickly than voters elsewhere and faulting him more harshly over the war in Lebanon.
With Iran demanding an end to Israel's military campaign as part of any peace deal it agrees with the United States, the poll shows how Netanyahu is increasingly caught between domestic electoral considerations and the diplomatic efforts of his allies in Washington.
The general election due by October could tip Netanyahu's governing coalition from power, risking his long record as Israel's arch political survivor.
While his government is widely seen as the most right-wing in Israeli history, many northern voters want a tougher military stance, unfettered by US pressure to end the conflicts in the Middle East.
For residents of the northern city of Kiryat Shmona, where some half of voters backed Likud in the last election, ending the threat from Hizbollah and its near-daily rocket and drone attacks is the biggest issue.
When sirens start to wail, they have only seconds to seek shelter and voters there told Reuters they want the war against Hizbollah pursued until the group is dismantled.
"All night there are loud explosions," said Kiryat Shmona resident Moshe Yifrah, 45, adding that he does not believe a ceasefire with Hizbollah would protect his family.
"Who would we make it with? Murderers who want to kill us?" he said.
Hizbollah began firing into Israel after the Hamas attack of Oct 7, 2023, and Israel waged an intense armed campaign in Lebanon, killing most of the group's leaders and forcing it to accept a ceasefire.
However, Hizbollah fired again after Israel and the United States launched a war on Iran on Feb 28, prompting Israel to renew its assault and seize swathes of south Lebanon.
More than 50 civilians have been killed by Hizbollah fire in north Israel since October 2023, according to Tel Aviv University's Institute for National Security Studies.
In Lebanon, more than 7,500 people have been killed by Israeli military action since October 2023, statements by Lebanese officials that do not distinguish between civilians and combatants show.
[[nid:1455302]]
Many northern voters, like Yifrah, want Israel to intensify its campaign, which continued despite an April truce, but believe Netanyahu is buckling to pressure from US President Donald Trump to agree a ceasefire.
"I'm not ashamed to say that I voted for this government, but it turns out that the one managing it is President Trump," said Yifrah.
Trump, who wants a deal with Iran, said on Monday ‌that Israel and Hizbollah had agreed to de-escalate hours after Netanyahu ordered new strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs.
Netanyahu's electoral rivals almost immediately accused him of compromising on national security - adding to his political difficulties months before the election is due.
"Everywhere Hizbollah is deployed it must be struck and the hands of the IDF should not be tied," said former military chief of staff Gadi Eizenkot, a prime ministerial hopeful, in a speech on Monday.
Then on Wednesday evening, a new truce agreement was struck requiring Hizbollah to leave south Lebanon. Netanyahu said soon afterwards that despite the ceasefire military operations would continue for now.
The Agam Labs poll showed only 23 per cent of voters in the north saying they would back Likud in the next election, down from the 35 per cent it gained in the last election in 2022.
Support for the wider right-wing bloc that makes up Netanyahu's coalition has fallen yet further in the north, the poll showed.
The drop in support for Likud is around three times greater in the north, home to around a fifth of the electorate, than elsewhere in Israel and around 70 per cent of the voters surveyed there said they disapproved of the handling of the war in Lebanon - more than elsewhere in Israel.
"We see a dramatic shift," said Nimrod Nir of Agam Labs.
"It's almost a mirror image of what we saw regarding the past elections, with two thirds intending to vote for the anti-Netanyahu bloc," he added.
The chairperson of Kiryat Shmona's Likud branch did not respond to a request for comment on the party's reduced support in polls or agree to an interview.
Nestled amid lush green mountains, Kiryat Shmona had been a prosperous centre of tourism and farming but residents now describe it as a ghost town with many residents having left.
Shops were shuttered and a playground was empty during a Reuters visit this week.
Netanyahu's main rivals are trying a hawkish message in the north, with Eizenkot visiting more than 15 times in recent weeks. Netanyahu has stayed away.
"He should come visit," said Yisrael Cohen, 40, who previously backed Likud but will not in the next election. "The government needs to see us."
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Couple plead not guilty to abusing three-year-old girl
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A husband and wife pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing a three-year-old girl, causing her physical injuries, last month.
Nightclub bouncer Hamzi Amni Zamali, 40, and housewife Nurazwani Ahmad Rosli, 29, were jointly charged, as persons responsible for the child's care, with abusing the girl and causing physical injuries at a low-cost flat unit in Bandar Tasik Selatan here on May 23, 2026.
The charge was framed under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001, read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum fine of RM50,000, imprisonment of up to 20 years, or both, upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Ravindejit Kaur objected to bail, citing the seriousness of the offence involving a child who is Nurazwani's biological daughter.
However, should the court allow bail, she proposed bail of RM30,000 for each accused, with an additional condition requiring them to report to the nearest police station twice a month.
"For the court's information, the first accused (Hamzi Amni) has several previous criminal records, including rape, robbery and armed robbery. Therefore, the prosecution requests that the victim in this case not live with the two accused," she said.
Hamzi Amni and Nurazwani, who were unrepresented, appealed for a lower bail amount, saying they had family members in their hometown to support.
Judge Siti Shakirah Mohtarudin granted bail of RM20,000 each with one surety and imposed additional conditions requiring them to report to the nearest police station twice a month and prohibiting them from approaching the victim until the case is concluded.
The court set July 13 for mention of the case, submission of documents and appointment of counsel. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR leader defends MyKhas portal ban for MPs linked to Rafizi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A senior PKR leader has defended the suspension of two party members of parliament from the MyKhas portal, linking the move to former deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.
PKR deputy secretary-general Bryan Ng said the suspension was a direct consequence of Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung and Subang MP Wong Chen's "self-inflicted betrayal".
"You cannot actively choose to align with a platform built to overthrow the government and also demand that the very same government hand over state resources.
"This is a fundamental matter of principles, discipline, and basic political accountability," Ng said in a statement.
Wong had earlier alleged that his office was
denied access to the MyKhas portal
without prior notice, affecting applications for constituency allocations.
Lee subsequently
lost access to the portal
, becoming the second PKR MP affected.
The dispute stems from both MPs' attendance at the May 17 launch of Parti Bersama Malaysia, the party
Rafizi and former PKR vice-president Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad
took over after quitting PKR.
"It is politically absurd to demand that the unity government channel federal funds through lawmakers who are openly working toward its defeat," Ng said.
He said constituency allocations were public funds belonging to the rakyat and could not be treated as a political tool by lawmakers working to destabilise the government from within.
Ng said Lee's victory in Petaling Jaya was built on the Pakatan Harapan brand and the work of local party machinery, adding that the first-term MP had come to the constituency as a relatively unknown former Pahang state assemblyman.
"You won Petaling Jaya purely because of the PH flag stamped next to your name and the tireless work of local party members who knocked on doors and guarded polling stations for you," he said.
On Wong, Ng said the veteran MP had long adopted the stance of an internal critic while enjoying the security of a government-aligned seat.
He pointed to Wong's public criticism of Putrajaya on social media following the end of his tenure as chairman of Malaysia Debt Ventures on May 31 as an example of
conduct unbecoming of a government backbencher
.
"You cannot act like an opposition insurgent and also demand the state resources reserved for a loyal government backbencher," he said.
He said those who wished to function as opposition lawmakers were free to do so through independent fundraising, but not through the administrative machinery and public funds of a government they were actively working against.
Despite the portal suspension, Ng said welfare initiatives and infrastructure projects in both constituencies would continue uninterrupted.
"The welfare of the people of Subang and Petaling Jaya remains safe, secure and fully prioritised.
The only thing being disrupted is individual political manoeuvring," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 3,208 transport job opportunities opened for military veterans
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A collaboration between the Transport Ministry and the Defence Ministry is set to create 3,208 job opportunities in the transport sector.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative aims to help armed forces personnel transition into civilian careers after completing their military service.
He said the collaboration between the two entities would create job opportunities for future retirees and veterans, while streamlining the conversion process of military driving licences to the relevant civilian licences.
"Many armed forces personnel have years of experience handling various types of vehicles and transport assets.
"We want to ensure that the skills they have acquired throughout their service can be translated into job opportunities more easily, without unnecessary bureaucracy.
"When this process is simplified, more veterans will be able to enter the transport sector quickly and meet the growing needs of the industry," he said.
He was speaking at the strategic cooperation ceremony between the transport and defence ministries for the empowerment of Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) among armed forces personnel here today.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi officiated the event, which was also attended by Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin.
At a press conference later, Loke said military truck drivers under Mindef already hold military licences, which would be converted to a Class E licence to operate heavy vehicles.
"We are now implementing this programme where conversion is done directly.
"They do not need to go through the usual process, which saves costs.
"They can obtain the licence immediately upon retirement, but there is also a transition period where they will be placed with logistics companies for training," he said.
Meanwhile, Khaled said personnel would continue receiving salaries from Mindef as they are still in service and have yet to retire.
After the training period, they would then be employed by companies and receive salaries on top of their monthly pension.
Khaled said around 5,000 military personnel retire each year, with more than 4,000 of them aged 39 and above.
"Various issues and challenges will arise, including the fact that at that age, their pensions may not be sufficient.
"It is also a loss for the country, as in a rapidly developing nation, we require a skilled and experienced workforce.
"Through the Ex-Servicemen Affairs Corporation (Perhebat), the ministry is reforming efforts to ensure that future military retirees undergo training during their transition period so that upon completion, they can secure employment," he said.
On March 16, Loke had said the government would implement a
special programme
for military retirees and veterans to obtain a class E licence to drive heavy vehicles.
He had said the new initiative was considered necessary to meet the shortage of heavy vehicle drivers in the haulage and logistics industry.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Armizan: 8,862 vehicles registered under expanded SKDS, SKPS as of June 3
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A total of 8,862 vehicles have been registered under the enhanced implementation of the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) and Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS), which took effect on June 1.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the figure comprised 8,524 diesel-powered pick-up trucks, 329 diesel jeeps and nine petrol jeeps registered between June 1 and 3.
He said the expanded eligibility under SKDS and SKPS now covers pick-up trucks and jeeps used in the land freight transport sector, subject to conditions including registration under a company or business entity and classification as commercial vehicles in the Road Transport Department's (JPJ) Motor Vehicle Licence (LKM) records.
"The expansion covers pick-up trucks and jeeps under the Rigid Goods and Rigid Goods Decon categories with a laden weight of between 950 kilogrammes and 7,500 kilogrammes.
"Vehicles registered under government, local authority and other special categories, as well as goods vehicles without permits or designated for off-road use, are not eligible," he said in a statement today.
Armizan said the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) had also revised the fixed quota limits under SKDS for the goods transportation sector to strengthen regulatory controls and curb subsidy leakages and misuse of fleet cards.
Under the revision, each fleet card will be allocated a monthly quota of between 900 litres and 5,000 litres, depending on the limits set for 23 eligible vehicle categories under SKDS.
"The quota levels were determined based on an analysis of actual fuel consumption data recorded over nearly two years of SKDS implementation since 2024," he said.
Companies or business entities requiring fuel volumes beyond the prescribed quota may submit appeals to the Petroleum Subsidy Approval Committee (JKSP), supported by operational justifications and at least three months of actual usage records.
Armizan said eight companies had submitted quota appeal applications involving 82 vehicles between June 1 and 3, and the requests were being processed for consideration by the committee.
He said KPDN would continue refining the implementation of SKDS and SKPS to ensure fuel subsidies are channelled in a targeted manner while curbing leakages, abuse and smuggling. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia passes bill expanding central bank role to spur growth
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's parliament on Thursday passed sweeping legislation that doubles down on Bank Indonesia's role to support growth, while empowering lawmakers to make binding recommendations for independent financial regulators and the central bank.
The bill has added to investor concerns about the possibility of political interference in the central bank to ensure compliance with President Prabowo Subianto's big-growth agenda, as his administration battles challenges on multiple fronts and sticks unswervingly to his target of eight per cent gross domestic product (GDP) growth by 2029.
The bill, backed a parliament overwhelmingly controlled by Prabowo's coalition, has not been made public, but some elements were discussed at a hearing on Wednesday, including legislators evaluating independent bodies and making binding recommendations for them.
Those include the central bank, Financial Services Authority and Indonesia Deposit Insurance Corporation.
Also in the bill was a new mechanism for removing members of the central bank's board of governors, Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa told Wednesday's hearing, without giving details. Purbaya has pledged to uphold central bank independence. A spokesperson for Bank Indonesia did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the bill.
CLOSE SCRUTINY ON POLICYMAKING
Its passage comes as investors cool on Indonesia, a G20 economy of US$1.4 trillion, with Moody's and Fitch this year cutting their credit rating outlooks to negative from stable, citing reduced policymaking credibility and predictability.
Indonesia's stock market has plunged over 30 per cent year to date and the rupiah has lost more than seven per cent against the US dollar this year, making it among the worst-performing emerging Asian currencies. It hit a historic low of 18,045 per dollar on Thursday.
Bhima Yudhistira Adhinegara, executive director of the Center of Economic and Law Studies think tank, said the bill could challenge central bank independence and effectively allow politicians to influence independent bodies.
"The most concerning part is on the mechanism to remove members of the central bank's board of governors ... which could be thick with political intent," Bhima said.
"A governor or an executive that doesn't align with political pressures could be removed," he said, adding he had yet to see the new bill.
Purbaya on Thursday said financial system reforms were necessary and the government was committed to achieving higher and sustainable economic growth.
"Indonesia's economy requires a breakthrough in several sectors, including in a healthy and sturdy financial sector," he added.
EXPANDED CENTRAL BANK MANDATE
Purbaya had on Wednesday said the bill adds creating "an economic environment conducive to real sector growth and job creation" to the central bank's policy goals. Bank Indonesia officials have repeatedly said economic growth is already considered in its policymaking.
Proponents of the bill have defended the changes, pointing to a similar employment mandate held by the US Federal Reserve. CELIOS' Bhima said the growth emphasis risked tilting Bank Indonesia's policy towards a looser monetary setting over being more "pro-stability", but noted its recent 50-basis-point rate hike suggested resistance from possible interference.
Also included are rules on sovereign wealth fund Danantara's bond issuance, including sale of special bonds like its controversial Patriot bonds and "red-and-white" bonds, Purbaya said on Wednesday, without elaborating.
The legislation also covers a plan to demutualise the Indonesia Stock Exchange, rules regarding a bourse for minerals and strategic commodities, and rules on a proposed creation of an international financial centre.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Queen visits Chow Kit school for   underprivileged children
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, visited Sekolah Bimbingan Jalinan Kasih (SBJK) for underprivileged children today as part of the king's birthday celebrations.
According to a post on the  Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page, the queen was received by Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and senior deputy secretary-general of the Prime Minister's Department Datuk Abd Shukor Mahmood.
The queen was briefed on the role of the Education Ministry, as well as SBJK's operations and achievements.
The Education Ministry established the school in 2013, with the aspiration of providing education for all.
SBJK currently provides formal education to 158 children who are marginalised or orphans in the Chow Kit area here.
Raja Zarith Sofiah also presented a contribution to the school and spent time interacting with its pupils and teachers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Banking sector's loan quality in focus as fuel costs bite
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The next few months will be key for banks as investors watch for any rise in bad loans that could trigger higher provisions.
Kenanga Research said while gross impaired loans remain healthy at 1.4 per cent, banks are waiting for clearer signs of how higher fuel prices affect borrowers, with any weakness likely to emerge in the second quarter.
"We anticipate the coming months to be crucial points of assessment for the banking sector's health, particularly with regards to GIL ratios, which if that were to creep up could spell greater provisioning needs for the banks.
"Still, even SME-concentrated banks like Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd and AMMB Holdings Bhd remain confident that delinquencies will remain manageable in the near term, supported by existing overlay buffers and the absence of meaningful stress signals thus far," it said.
Kenanga Research said the cumulative loan loss coverage (LLC) for its coverage of 10 listed banks stands at 89.8 per cent, well above the industry average of 83.0 per cent.
"This suggests that listed banks are generally better provisioned and that any weaker asset quality trends may be concentrated among institutions outside our coverage," it said.
Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said while energy-related shocks from Middle Eastern conflicts pose risks to retail and SME borrowers, domestic banks remain well cushioned by sizeable management overlays and high levels of collateral backing.
"Furthermore, Bank Negara Malaysia's late-April launch of the RM5 billion SME Stabilisation Relief Facility should insulate asset quality and prevent a spike in GILs.
"Given near-term macro uncertainties and investment sentiment volatility, we advocate a selective stock-picking strategy, favouring CIMB Group Holdings Bhd and AMMB for their undemanding valuations and superior dividend visibility," it said.
Both Kenanga Research and HLIB maintained an "Overweight" stance on the banking sector.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Road bully jailed, fined RM8k and banned from driving for five years
Article:
BALIK PULAU: A man who went viral after being caught on video making an obscene gesture against a fellow motorist was sentenced to a day's jail and fined RM8,000 by the Magistrate's Court here today.
Tan Chee Gang, 37, was also barred from holding a driving licence for five years, after pleading guilty to two charges involving making obscene gestures and dangerous driving.
Tan admitted to both offences, which were committed along Jalan Tun Dr Awang at 11.27am on May 28.
For the first charge, Tan was fined RM3,000 in default of six months' jail after he was found to have made an obscene gesture — raising his middle finger — towards Omar Che Din, 59, with the intention of provoking fear and insulting him.
He was charged under Section 507B of the Penal Code, which provides for a maximum jail term of three years, or a fine, or both upon conviction.
For the second charge, Tan was sentenced to a day's jail and fined RM5,000 in default of six months' jail after he pleaded guilty to driving his car in a dangerous manner.
He was charged under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Amendment 1999), which carries a maximum penalty of five years' imprisonment and a fine of up to RM15,000.
Magistrate Chia Huey Ting, in meting out the sentence, also ordered Tan to be disqualified from holding or obtaining a driving licence for five years.
Deputy public prosecutor, Ikmal Affandi Zulkifli, appeared for the prosecution while Tan was represented by a lawyer from the National Legal Aid Foundation.
The prosecution urged the court to impose an appropriate sentence as a deterrent to the accused and the public against aggressive and dangerous behaviour on the road.
Tan, through his legal counsel, sought a minimum sentence.
"My client earns about RM80 a day as a lorry driver and has to support his parents. He also expressed remorse for his actions," the counsel added.
It is understood that Tan did not pay the fines. The court then ordered imprisonment in default of payment.
Tan was previously arrested after a 58-second video of the incident went
viral
on social media, showing a driver overtaking a vehicle abruptly before making the obscene gesture.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia draws RM250bil in digital investments in two years: Akmal Nasrullah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has attracted RM250 billion in approved digital investments over the past two years, with the projects expected to create around 80,000 skilled jobs.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the investment reflected strong investor confidence in Malaysia's economic prospects and its push into high-growth sectors such as artificial intelligence (AI), semiconductors and data centres.
The government aims to leverage the momentum to accelerate economic restructuring and move Malaysia further up the value chain, he added.
The government is also seeking to maximise the benefits of private-sector investments by ensuring they contribute to talent development, technology transfer and long-term competitiveness.
"We have to take advantage of the investment momentum into to Malaysia and ensure that these private investments contribute to improving our competitiveness.
"We have to ensure that incoming investments support the upskilling of our workforce and provide greater access to advanced technologies. This will be key to helping Malaysia navigate the challenges and concerns we are facing," he said during a ministerial dialogue at the Energy Transition Conference 2026 (ETCon26) here today.
Akmal said Malaysia must move beyond traditional sector-based development and adopt a more integrated approach linking energy, digitalisation and economic growth.
According to him, energy infrastructure has become a critical enabler of economic activity, particularly as emerging technologies such as AI and data centres require substantial electricity and water resources.
"Energy leads to digitalisation because today's digital economy is energy intensive. Digitalisation then uplifts productivity, and from productivity we should aim for greater economic complexity and better incomes for our citizens."
He said the country's energy transition agenda should not be viewed solely through the lens of decarbonisation but also as a catalyst for economic transformation.
Malaysia, he said, is focusing on sectors with strong growth potential, including semiconductors, AI and data centres, while seeking to deepen participation in higher-value activities.
In the semiconductor industry, Akmal said the government's objective is to move beyond traditional packaging and testing activities into advanced packaging and integrated circuit (IC) design.
He cited Malaysia's partnership with British semiconductor intellectual property firm Arm Ltd as part of efforts to strengthen domestic capabilities and position the country higher in the global semiconductor value chain.
"The government decided to enter into a partnership with Arm to move beyond semiconductor packaging and testing.
"While we are still advancing into more sophisticated packaging technologies, our ambition goes further towards integrated circuit (IC) design.
"It is now up to us — not just the government, but also the industry — to take this opportunity to uplift the standard," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gunung Batu Putih closed off to climbers following Jaslinda's disappearance
Article:
IPOH: Gunung Batu Putih in Tapah is now temporarily closed to the public following the disappearance of hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who is still missing.
Perak Forestry Department director, Datuk Mohd Basri Abdul Manaf, said the closure of the climbing trail, expected to be between three and six months, is to enable the authorities to study the safety aspects and repair works along the route.
"The mountain is closed temporarily as we need to assess its safety aspects and possible repairs. This could take anywhere between three and six months," he said.
Mohd Basri was speaking to reporters after attending the Perak-level 2026 International Forestry Day at the
Dewan Bankuet Bangunan Perak Darul Ridzuan, here today.
Also present were Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad and Peninsular Malaysia Forestry director-general Datuk Zahari Ibrahim.
Meanwhile, Saarani said the local Orang Asli community should be involved in any forest-based search and rescue operation, including those in permanent forest reserves.
This, he said, was because the community know the ins and outs of the forests near their areas, and to avoid the community's agricultural land from being accidentally damaged by SAR personnel.
"There have been occasions where the Orang Asli have complained that they were not told beforehand about the large number of hikers entering their area. In some SAR missions, rescuers enter their area and accidentally destroy their crops.
"By right, any SAR mission should involve the Orang Asli, especially the Tok Batin (village head)."
The search for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, who disappeared on May 24, only saw the involvement of villagers from the Kampung Orang Asli Ampang Woh last Tuesday, more than a week after the SAR operation was launched.
Meanwhile, Saarani said the state already has specific regulations for climbing activities, such as the minimum climbing period based on a peak's height.
However, he said the regulations need to be reviewed as it should not only be time-based, but also dependent on the climber's fitness levels.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Unlicensed welfare home caretaker gets 36 years for sexual abuse of minors
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The caretaker of an unlicensed children's welfare home who sexually assaulted five boys has been sentenced to 36 years' jail and six strokes of the rotan by the Ampang Sessions Court.
Judge Ho Kwong Chin sentenced Retna Velu, 25, after he maintained his guilty plea to nine charges involving physical and non-physical sexual abuse.
Eight of the charges involved physical sexual assault, while one involved non-physical sexual assault.
The victims were aged eight, 10, 11, 12 and 13.
He received jail terms ranging from five to 12 years on each charge, with the sentences ordered to run consecutively from his arrest on April 3.
Retna, dressed in a prison outfit in the dock, appeared calm during the proceedings.
Unrepresented, he appealed for leniency.
"I plead guilty and I ask for a light sentence," he said.
However, deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Hairuliqram Hairuddin pressed for a stiff penalty due to the gravity of the offences and the impact on the victims.
"He has violated the children's dignity and severely affected their well-being.
"His heinous acts will have a profound and lasting impact on the victims for the rest of their lives.
"A proportionate sentence must be imposed to reflect the seriousness of his actions," he said.
Retna committed the offences in November last year, February and March at the home at Taman Permata Biru in Batu Caves, Gombak.
The physical sexual assault charges were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Crimes Against Children Act, punishable under Section 14 which carries a maximum 20 year jail term and whipping upon conviction.
The non-physical sexual assault were framed under Section 15(a)(ii) of the same act which carries a maximum 10-year jail term or a fine not exceeding RM20,000 or both upon conviction.
The court heard that some of the boys had been placed at the home after experiencing bullying and because their mother was unable to care for them while working.
One of the boys had been living at the home since he was very young.
Most of the assaults were allegedly carried out in the early hours of the morning, with Retna reportedly instructing one of the victims to keep the abuse a secret.
The boys were rescued from the home and taken to Selayang Hospital on March 30, where medical specialists discovered anal injuries consistent with abuse.
While being held at the Selayang police station lock-up, Retna admitted to committing the acts against the children.
On May 21, Sessions Court judge Norshila Kamaruddin sentenced Retna to 11-years and five strokes of the rotan after pleading
guilty
to six sexual offences, including committing carnal intercourse against the order of nature involving two boys.
However, as the sentences will run concurrently, he will serve a
jail term
of 36 years.
The case came to light after the president of Pertubuhan Kebangkitan Tiga Tangan Selangor spoke with several children, who disclosed the alleged abuse.
Lawyers Kirthen Subramaniam and Premila Subramaniam held a watching brief on behalf of the victim's families and the organisation, which exposed the alleged decade long abuse at the unlicensed home.
The New Straits Times had reported that at least 12 out of 36 occupants of the home had been subjected to abuse.
Police arrested and remanded four suspects, including the home's 31-year-old owner and three workers aged between 17 and 25.
It was found that the home was not registered with the Social Welfare Department and operated under a private company.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippines AirAsia settles outstanding airport dues
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) today confirms that Philippines AirAsia has paid the outstanding airport charges owed to the regulator today.
"CAAP confirms that Philippines AirAsia has complied with the directive to settle its obligations this morning, June 4, subject to ongoing standard reconciliation processes," CAAP said in a statement today when contacted by Business Times.
The Philippines aviation regulator said it acknowledges and appreciates Philippines AirAsia's commitment to addressing its obligations through constructive engagement and coordination with CAAP.
"CAAP remains committed to working closely with all aviation stakeholders to ensure regulatory compliance, maintain the stability of air transport operations and safeguard the interests of the travelling public," it added in the statement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TSMC boss bets big on AI growth, says he'd 'like' to hike chip prices
Article:
HSINCHU: Taiwan's TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, is confident in its growth over the next few years, thanks to robust demand for computing power and advanced semiconductors as it rides a relentless AI boom, its chief executive officer (CEO) said on Thursday.
CC Wei, speaking at TSMC's annual shareholders' meeting in the Taiwanese city of Hsinchu, said customers are still positive on the outlook for artificial intelligence, although the company continues to monitor the impact of rising component costs.
"We continue to see increasing adoption of AI models across consumer, enterprise and sovereign AI applications," Wei said, adding that while the company is working very hard, demand is high and it can only produce so much.
"This trend is driving demand for greater computing power, which in turn supports strong demand for advanced semiconductor chips."
Asked whether TSMC could raise prices for customers, Wei said: "I'd like to do that ... we still need to make money."
"We don't want to suddenly raise prices like memory companies do. That's not sustainable. TSMC is focused on long-term, sustainable operations. We're not that kind of company."
Taiwan plays a crucial role in the global AI supply chain for companies including Nvidia and Apple, and its position is anchored by TSMC.
The Taiwanese tech giant said it was working hard to meet all customer demands, although it will take a "very long time" to fully satisfy American customers' needs with production in the US, he said, without elaborating on a time frame.
TSMC is investing US$165 billion to build new factories in the US state of Arizona, where Wei said the company's two parcels of land there should be sufficient for the next 10 years.
He said TSMC's stock performance over the past year had been remarkable, with its share price climbing to T$2,425 by Wednesday, up from T$950 on June 3 last year.
Its shares were down more than one per cent on Thursday, in line with the benchmark index.
Asked whether TSMC wanted to invest in ASML's next-generation High-NA EUV machines, which can cost up to US$400 million each, Wei said his company had purchased the Dutch firm's High-NA tools and was conducting R&D efforts. The machines are advanced lithography systems that are used to transfer intricate patterns onto silicon wafers to form integrated circuits and other devices.
"We do not currently need it for production because the cost is still somewhat high," he said. "We will continue working to lower the cost and maximize its benefits and once the economics make sense, we will bring it into production."
Amid recent worker tensions over pay in South Korea, where Samsung Electronics last month clinched a deal with its union to avert a strike, TSMC said it was fully committed to taking care of its employees, underscoring how the beneficiaries of a massive AI boom are coming under growing pressure to share more of their rapidly growing income.
"(Our) employee profit sharing increased by about 30 per cent from 2023 to 2024 and again by about 30 per cent from 2024 to 2025," he said. "We are confident it will rise by another 30 per cent in 2026."
"We believe this represents strong compensation for our employees," he said, adding that there is no ceiling for 30 per cent annual growth in terms of employee profit sharing as it will continue to grow.
Looking ahead, Wei said he views autonomous vehicles as long-term growth drivers and the company will be working hard to ensure robots can succeed. Taiwan remains TSMC's most efficient manufacturing area, which is home to its best talent, core R&D and largest production base, he added.
Taiwan has grabbed the spotlight this week as it hosts the annual Computex conference, where leaders of some of the world's most powerful tech companies are gathering. Executives from the likes of Nvidia and Intel have heaped praise on the island's central role in the global supply chain.
In April, TSMC, a major Nvidia supplier, raised its annual revenue forecast and said it was stepping up capital spending this year to meet relentless demand for its products.
TSMC faces political risk as China steps up military pressure on democratically and separately governed Taiwan, which Beijing views as "sacred" Chinese territory.
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has held court in Taiwan over the past few days, dining with South Korean technology leaders and signing autographs for some of his fans, just weeks after accompanying US President Donald Trump on a visit to Beijing as part of a high-profile corporate delegation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teacher badly burned after gas cylinder explodes during leak check
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: A teacher suffered burns to 50 per cent of his face and hands after a gas cylinder exploded while he was investigating a suspected gas leak at his rented home in Kuala Ina yesterday.
In the 10.26am incident, Mohamad Edham Zul, 29, a teacher at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sultan Mansor Shah in Tampin, went to the kitchen after hearing what he believed was gas escaping from a cylinder.
As he was checking the source of the sound, the cylinder exploded.
Melaka Fire and Rescue Department public relations officer Deputy Fire Officer Mohd Hafidzatullah Rashid said Edham received emergency treatment at the scene before being taken to hospital.
He said firefighters also carried out inspections and monitored the area for further gas leak risks with the help of other agencies.
Hafidzatullah said the explosion caused extensive damage to the house, including its roof, doors and windows.
The victim's sister, Nurul Atiqah, 31, said Edham, the youngest of five siblings, was in stable condition and had been transferred to Kuala Lumpur Hospital for skin graft surgery.
Edham's wife and child were not injured in the incident, she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mexico's popular former president blasts Trump as US ties hit low
Article:
MEXICO CITY: Former Mexican President Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador accused Washington on Wednesday of employing "interventionist and unscrupulous practices" to bolster Mexico's right-wing opposition and weaken his leftist political movement.
The remarks come amid escalating tension between the United States and Mexico, now led by President Claudia Sheinbaum, whom Lopez Obrador also praised as the "best president Mexico has had in our time."
One of Mexico's most popular political figures, Lopez Obrador has largely stayed out of the spotlight since leaving office in 2024, but that changed with his forceful rebuke of US President Donald Trump posted on X on Wednesday.
Trump had changed since his first term, Lopez Obrador said in his five-page letter, describing the president then as cooperative, pragmatic and open to dialogue.
"Speaking from what I personally experienced and can prove, the Trump of today is different from the one I dealt with," he wrote.
He blamed Trump's transformation on what he called "false friends and advisers, both internal and external, who have led him into vile and sinister adventures."
Lopez Obrador holds no official power, but he retains significant political influence, and his remarks amount to a forceful show of support for Sheinbaum, his political protégé.
Mexican and US officials could not be immediately reached for comment about Lopez Obrador's remarks.
After more than a year of cooperative, if often strained, relations with Washington, Sheinbaum has sharpened her rhetoric in recent days, stepping up calls to defend national sovereignty.
The US justice department's April indictment of 10 Mexican officials over alleged drug trafficking ties has emerged as a particular sore point in relations.
Lopez Obrador accused US officials of seeking to undermine Mexico's ruling Morena party, which he founded, under the guise of combatting migration and narco-terrorism.
He concluded, "For the good of all, may the other Trump return."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Make Malaysia a hostile environment for scam syndicates
Article:
The Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation had identified at least two locations in Malaysia believed to be linked to scam syndicate operations, according to reports.
The groups were suspected of relocating their bases after enforcement agencies, including the United States Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) intensified operations against scam compounds in Southeast Asia.
We've also heard about Malaysians getting trapped in scam compounds in Myanmar, Cambodia and Laos. So it is deeply disturbing when we read in the media that such syndicates might be shifting operations here.
We need a National Anti-Scam Security Task Force. Bring together the police, immigration, customs, financial regulators, cyber experts, and intelligence agencies.
These syndicates operate as one unit, our response needs to be just as unified.
Tighten immigration controls because every suspicious influx of foreign workers — especially those tied to questionable industries or shell companies need to be investigated.
Scam operations survive because they can move and hide huge profits. Thus, banks and digital payment providers must flag suspicious transactions in real time.
Freeze assets linked to scams and confiscate them after conviction. These criminals fear losing their wealth more than they fear jail.
Get aggressive with telecom rules. Anonymous SIM cards, fake accounts, shady websites that's the lifeblood of scammers. We need rapid takedown systems, stricter verification, and AI monitoring.
So many people were promised good jobs abroad, only to end up trapped, exploited, abused. Every recruitment agency, every job ad, every overseas placement programme needs real scrutiny.
No organised to crime ring survives long without someone on the inside looking the other way. So we need integrity audits, strong whistleblower protection, and harsh penalties for any official caught helping these networks.
The public have a role too. Report suspicious activities, unusual businesses and  dubious job offers. Silence and indifference are just fertile ground for organised crime.
Our goal shouldn't be just to arrest scammers but to make Malaysia the most hostile place for these syndicates to operate, recruit, launder money, or corrupt anyone.
K. TAMIL MARAN
Seremban
Negri Sembilan
*The views expressed in this article are the author's own and do not necessarily reflect those of the New Straits Times
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israel far-right minister slams ceasefire with Lebanon as 'serious mistake'
Article:
TEL AVIV: Israel's far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir criticised on Thursday a ceasefire deal with Lebanon brokered by Washington, calling it a "serious mistake."
"The ceasefire with Lebanon is a serious mistake and the pipe dreams of advisers are dragging the prime minister (Benjamin Netanyahu) to wrong decisions," the minister wrote on X.
Israel and Lebanon agreed Wednesday to implement a ceasefire but said it would require a "complete cessation" of fire by Iran-backed Hizbollah, according to a joint statement after US-led talks in Washington.
Ben Gvir said the deal, which would see Hizbollah withdraw from southern Lebanon, cannot be enforced and the Lebanese state cannot be trusted.
"Hizbollah has not left the area south of the Litani, and the Lebanese army has no way to enforce its evacuation. The state of Lebanon is a partner of Hizbollah," the hawkish minister wrote.
He also chastised Netanyahu for giving in to US pressures to reach a deal.
"The prime minister should have told President Trump: We love and appreciate you, but Israel is a sovereign and independent state," Ben Gvir wrote.
"There are moments when one must know how to say 'no', even to the president of the United States, and when we don't do so, we will meet Hizbollah next time when it is much stronger and more dangerous," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Velesto Energy unit receives drilling contract in Thailand
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Velesto Energy Bhd's subsidiary Velesto Sumber Sdn Bhd has secured a contract from Northern Gulf Petroleum Pte Ltd for offshore drilling rig services in Thailand.
Under the contract, Velesto will deploy the Naga 6 rig for a drilling campaign in the Gulf of Thailand.
The scope of work includes the drilling of four infill wells and three exploration wells.
Velesto president Megat Zariman Abdul Rahim said the award supports the continued utilisation of the company's premium jack-up rigs.
He added that the contract is expected to contribute positively towards earnings.
"The contract award further strengthens Velesto's drilling segment and reflects continued ability to secure opportunities across our core operating markets amid ongoing offshore development and exploration activities in the region," he said in a statement.
He added that the award also adds to Velesto's existing order book and strengthens the utilisation of its jack-up rig fleet.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TNB, Petronas Gas sign pact for third LNG terminal project
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) has inked a heads of agreement with Petronas Gas Bhd to jointly develop the third regasification terminal (RGT-3) in Lumut, Perak.
This was signed through TNB's indirect wholly-owned subsidiary Integrax Bhd, a unit of TNB Power Generation Sdn Bhd.
TNB said the agreement serves as a framework for further discussions and negotiations between the parties to formalise the preliminary terms and respective investment participation in the proposed joint venture.
"A special purpose vehicle will be incorporated by both parties upon finalisation and signing of the shareholders' agreement and the regasification terminal will be co-developed by both parties through the vehicle," it said in a Bursa Malaysia filing.
The utility company said RGT-3 will be developed based on a floating storage regasification unit (FSRU) concept, under which liquefied natural gas (LNG) storage and regasification activities will be carried out onboard the vessel.
The regasified LNG will be transported through a connecting pipeline from RGT-3 onshore and berthing facilities to the peninsular gas utilisation system.
"RGT-3 is designed with LNG storage capacity of 170,000 m3 and regasification send-out capacity of 500 million standard cubic feet per day. The RGT-3 capacity is being underwritten by TNB Fuel Services Sdn Bhd in stages," TNB added.
TNB said the project will strengthen fuel security, cost competitiveness and long-term growth through the development of integrated LNG infrastructure.
"The RGT-3 project will position strategically beyond electricity into LNG infrastructure and broader gas value chain participation and advance co-development of RGT-3 as a critical regasification infrastructure asset with strategic partners," it said.
TNB said the development of RGT-3 is currently planned to be funded through a combination of debt and equity.
"While investment in the project may result in an increase in the group's gearing, it is not expected to have any material impact on the group's net assets per share for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: More oil escapes Hormuz, keeping traders guessing
Article:
THE trickle of tankers exiting the Strait of Hormuz has gathered pace in recent weeks, as traders adopt stealth measures to make the crossing.
While this is freeing some of the vast oil inventories trapped in the Gulf, it does not signal a slow return to normalcy. Instead, it previews the opaque, fragmented energy market the Iran war is set to leave in its wake.
More than four months into the conflict, the US and Iran are still struggling to hammer out an agreement to formally end the war and fully reopen the narrow waterway.
The near-total closure of Hormuz stranded more than 13 million barrels of oil per day within the Gulf, forcing producers to shut down oilfields and refineries, triggering supply shortfalls and economic strain across major importing nations.
Traffic through the strait remains a fraction of pre-war levels. On the face of it, an average of just three tankers a day has crossed in and out of Hormuz since the conflict began - roughly one-tenth of normal volumes - according to shipping monitors including LSEG and Kpler.
But a closer look at oil stocks tells a more nuanced story.
An analysis of the huge volumes stored on tankers inside the Gulf suggests transit activity has quietly accelerated. It points to a growing number of ships leaving the region "under the radar" of satellite tracking systems.
More vessels appear to be switching off their Automatic Identification System (AIS) before and after transiting the strait, adopting tactics long used by Iran to evade Western sanctions.
In practice, tankers can "go dark" for days around the crossing, only to reappear weeks later near their destination.
Shipping analytics firm Vortexa estimates that around 65 per cent of outbound laden tankers transited in "dark" mode in May, showing how widespread the practice has become.
That opacity is distorting the market's line of sight. Reduced visibility into cargo movements and destinations makes it harder to gauge the flows underpinning benchmark pricing.
That makes alternative indicators increasingly important.
One key gauge of the pace of outflows is "oil on water" in the Gulf, or the volume of oil stored on tankers trapped behind the strait.
Levels have dropped from a peak of 184 million barrels on March 22 to around 148 million barrels this week, according to Kpler data, implying an average drawdown rate of roughly 500,000 bpd.
Crucially, that pace has accelerated over the past month. Since the start of May, depletion has increased to around 710,000 bpd, according to ROI analysis.
That offers further evidence that flows out of the Gulf, while still constrained, are inching higher.
[[nid:1455219]]
Exactly which routes these dark tankers are taking remains unclear – as the term "dark" suggests.
Many are likely using corridors designated by Iran, which has allowed limited volumes through the strait under bilateral arrangements with Asian governments including Pakistan, India, China and Japan.
This underscores the region's heavy dependence on Gulf supply. Some vessels may be paying Iran a fee for safe passage.
Other vessels may be taking routes closer to Oman's coastline, potentially with the tacit or active support of the US Navy, which continues to play a stabilising role in regional maritime security.
The situation remains fluid and could shift quickly. Iran could tighten its grip on shipping at any moment, particularly if negotiations with the US continue to stall.
After more than three months of severe economic disruption, every barrel exported offers a lifeline to revenue-starved Gulf producers such as Iraq and Kuwait, and desperate buyers in Asia.
But a sustained recovery will require far greater clarity and stability around Hormuz.
Producers are unlikely to restart the roughly 11 million bpd of oilfields shut in during the conflict without confidence that exports can flow reliably.
One key constraint is logistical: the return of empty tankers to the Gulf. Without a steady inflow of vessels to load cargoes, onshore storage tanks will remain near capacity, preventing the restart of shut-in production.
That crucial rebalancing - laden ships leaving and empty ships returning - has yet to materialise at scale.
Shipowners and charterers remain wary of sending vessels into a region where the risk of becoming stranded remains high. Insurance premiums continue to reflect that elevated risk, reinforcing a cautious approach to redeploying fleets.
Ultimately, the market may never return to normal, even if a political breakthrough officially "reopens" the strait.
Tehran is seeking to retain control over traffic in the waterway and introduce a tolling system, potentially reshaping how one of the world's most critical oil chokepoints operates.
That would represent an untenable situation for Gulf producers, forcing them to find alternate routes. And if they can't erode Iran's position strategically, they may seek to do so militarily.
The shift toward a more opaque trading environment in the Middle East may be providing some marginal relief. But the fragmented, dangerous reality it reflects means any respite may be short-lived.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: State assembly dissolutions up to individual states [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the decision to dissolve a state assembly rests with the respective state government.
He said the government's priority at present is to fulfil its responsibilities rather than focus on elections.
Anwar was
responding
to questions on whether he planned to dissolve parliament to pave the way for the 16th General Election (GE16), following the dissolution of the Johor state assembly on Monday.
"That is their decision. We (the government) have our priorities. We are focused on our work (governing the country)," he said after launching the Energy Transition Conference at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre here today.
Under election laws, the Election Commission (EC) has up to 60 days from the date of dissolution to hold the state election.
The 16th Johor election is shaping up to feature four-cornered contests, with Barisan Nasional (BN), Pakatan Harapan (PH), Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Bersatu all signalling their intention to contest the 56 state seats.
Multi-cornered contests are expected in most constituencies, although some seats could see more than four candidates if smaller parties and independents enter the fray.
The election is also being closely watched as a key test of support for the BN-led Johor government, the unity government and the strength of the opposition ahead of the next general election.
In the 2022 state election, BN secured a commanding victory, winning 40 of the 56 seats.
PH holds 12 seats, PN three and Muda one.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian rupiah falls to record low against US dollar
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's rupiah hit more than 18,000 per dollar for the first time Thursday as the country is stung by surging energy costs, while lawmakers passed a bill expanding oversight of the central bank that raised concerns over its independence.
Bank Indonesia has in recent weeks taken several measures to try and bolster the currency by hiking interest rates, which has been hammered this year amid mounting concerns about the state of Southeast Asia's biggest economy.
But on Thursday morning, the unit broke the psychological 18,000 barrier to hit 18,047 against the greenback. The country's stock market sank nearly four percent and has lost a third of its value in 2026.
That came hours before parliament gave the green light to a legislative amendment that expands the bank's mandate to include responsibility for economic growth, but also allows lawmakers to evaluate its performance.
The parliamentary oversight will also apply to the Indonesia Deposit Insurance Corporation (LPS), tasked with guaranteeing bank deposits and ensuring banking system stability, and the Financial Services Authority (OJK) financial regulator.
The vote came a day after Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa told parliament the legislative amendment was aimed at boosting economic growth and enhancing global competitiveness as the economy is hit by a spike in global oil prices.
"It's not just about exchange rate stability, or just about inflation. It's also about paying attention to economic growth and creating jobs," he said.
Economist Yose Rizal Damuri, of the Centre for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), said the move raised the "question of independence", especially if parliamentary intervention becomes "routine."
"Amid global geopolitical pressures, the market needs certainty that monetary policy, financial supervision and market stabilisation remain independent and free from political intervention," added Central Bank Asia chief economist David Sumual.
"If independence is perceived to be weakening, the risk could trigger an increase in risk premiums and put pressure on financial markets," he told AFP.
- 'Costly populist programmes' -
The rupiah has tumbled more than seven percent this year and has been Asia's worst-performing currency, according to Bloomberg News, as the US-Israel war on Iran sent global oil prices surging.
Permata Bank chief economist Josua Pardede said an exchange rate of 18,000 was a "psychological threshold" for market investors.
The weakening, he told AFP, was fuelled by high dollar demand caused by the surge in oil prices and a narrowing trade surplus.
Indonesia is a net oil importer and is particularly impacted by the rising crude costs, though the government insists it will leave subsidised fuel prices unchanged.
The country's trade surplus has been hammered, narrowing to just $89 million in April, from $3.3 billion a month before, further reducing dollar supply in the Indonesian market, Josua said.
"Dollar supply from goods trade is dwindling, while dollar needs for energy imports, raw materials, dividends, foreign debt payments and seasonality needs remain significant," he told AFP on Wednesday.
"This is why the increase in the BI (Bank Indonesia) lending rate and intervention is not enough to reverse the rupiah's (depreciation)."
The central bank hiked rates by 0.5 basis points to 5.25 percent last month – the first increase in two years – as it looked to stabilise the rupiah and keep inflation in check.
It has also tightened rules for dollar purchases. Since May, buyers of more than $25,000 in a given month must provide supporting documents to justify their need for greenbacks.
The level was previously lowered from $100,000 to $50,000 in April.
Teuku Riefky, a University of Indonesia economist, said the rupiah was reacting not only to the economic fallout of the Middle East war, but also "various costly populist programmes" including the government's multi-billion dollar school feeding scheme.
There was also flagging confidence among investors in Jakarta's ability to repay its debts.
"If this continues, the impact will be sustained inflation due to rising production and raw material costs," Teuku told AFP.
Further fuelling investor concerns, President Prabowo Subianto last month announced commodity export controls that set markets aflutter amid concerns over "resource nationalism" in the world's largest palm oil producer.
